prompt:
I want to design a HARMONi-inspired phase 3 oncology trial, where two geographic populations enroll on different timelines.

This is not intended to exactly reproduce the HARMONi SAP. Please treat it as a synthetic case study inspired by HARMONi.

Trial setting:
- Two-arm randomized phase 3 trial
- Randomization: 1:1 treatment vs control
- Endpoints: PFS and OS. Overall onesided alpha 0.025.  IA1: PFS primary analysis at one sided alpha = 0.0025. If passed, pass the 0.0025 alpha to OS as OS interim. FA: OS primary analysis
- Primary analysis population: pooled Population A + Population B
- Treatment effect may differ by population
- Assume exponential PFS and OS distributions unless otherwise specified


HARMONi-inspired enrollment assumptions:
- Population A starts enrollment at month 0
- Population A enrolls approximately 60% patients
- Population A accrual duration is approximately 10–11 months
- Population A accrual rate is approximately 25 patients/month
- Population B starts enrollment at month 16
- Population B enrolls approximately 40% patients
- Population B accrual duration is approximately 17–18 months
- Population B accrual rate is approximately 9–10 patients/month
- Include 5% annual non-informative dropout for PFS and OS


Endpoint assumptions:

Population A:
- Median PFS control = 4.8 months
- Median PFS treatment = 7.1 months
- Median OS control = 14.0 months
- Median OS treatment = 17.0 months

Population B:
- Median PFS control = 3.8 months
- Median PFS treatment = 6.2 months
- Median OS control = 14.0 months
- Median OS treatment = 17.0 months
expected_output:
The recommended design should have the following characteristics:
- Achieves at least 80% power for PFS FA and OS FA
- Also report joint probability of success for both PFS and OS
- If it is difficult to achieve 80% joint power under the assumed OS effect size, show the sample size/event count needed and explain the trade-off
- Ensures Population B has mature enough PFS follow-up by the PFS FA / OS IA readout
- Ensures Population B has meaningful OS maturity by OS FA
- Avoids a design where pooled OS success is driven almost entirely by early-enrolling Population A
- Is robust to alternative staggered-enrollment patterns, including slower Population B accrual, earlier Population B activation
assertions: an approximate last-patient-in lag of 23 months between Population A and Population B
Population B median PFS follow-up >= 6 months AND at least 40% of expected Population B PFS events have occurred
Population B median OS follow-up >= 12 months AND at least 40% of expected Population B OS events have occurred
Population B contributes >=30% of total OS events at OS FA
IA timing should after LPI of population B. If not, explain.
The design is robust to weaker Western OS assumptions.
The design reports Population A and Population B follow-up separately.
